title: Garm Bel Iblis's movement
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Organization |image= |name=Garm Bel Iblis's movement |type=Resistance group |founder=[[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]]''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' |leader=*Garm Bel Iblis *[[Irenez (Human)|Irenez]]''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'' |subgroup= |headquarters=*[[Peregrine's Nest]] **[[Womrik]] **[[Mattri asteroid base|Mattri Asteroid]] **[[Churba sector/Legends|Churba sector]] |locations= |formed=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |founded=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved=[[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] {{C|joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]}} |restored= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|You're wondering why I didn't join the Alliance. Why I chose instead to run my own private war against the Empire.|Garm Bel Iblis, to Lando Calrissian|}} '''Garm Bel Iblis's movement''' was comprised of the remnants of the [[Corellian Resistance/Legends|Corellian Resistance]] that remained loyal to [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] following his rift with [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and subsequent split from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], shortly after the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction of Alderaan]]. Despite lacking [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] comparable with those of the [[Rebel Alliance Navy|Alliance Fleet]], Bel Iblis commandeered a number of successful raids on Imperial strongholds in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], and remained a thorn in the Empire's side for nearly a decade. Not until the [[Thrawn campaign]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], however, did his resistance group reconcile with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. ==History== [[File:CorellianTreaty-TFUComic.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Bel Iblis (on the left) at the signing of the Corellian Treaty.]] As the leader of the Corellian Resistance, Bel Iblis brought considerable weight to the table when joining forces with the [[Chandrilan resistance|Chandrilans]] and [[Alderaanian Resistance|Alderaanians]] in the [[Corellian Treaty]] of [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. In years prior, the Resistance had been active throughout the Corellian system, planting undercover operatives such as [[Bria Tharen]] in Imperial military facilities, whose gathered intelligence was relayed to leadership through handlers, including [[Rion (individual)|Rion]].''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'' All the while, in his official capacity as [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] of Corellia, Bel Iblis was traversing the Galaxy, rallying against the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. This came to an end in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], when an attempt on his life was made at the [[Treitamma Political Center]] in [[Anchoron]], killing Bel Iblis' wife [[Arrianya Bel Iblis|Arrianya]] and their two children, and ostensibly the Senator, too.{{StoryCite|story=Interlude at Darkknell|book=Tales from the New Republic}} Bel Iblis, however, had survived the attack through sheer coincidence, as he was contacted by [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa's]] emissary, codenamed "[[Aach]]", just moments before the bomb at the Treitamma Center exploded. Understanding that the Empire would not shy away from another assassination attempt, Bel Iblis went into hiding and was publicly believed to be dead. ===Withdrawal from the Rebel Alliance=== {{Quote|Do you understand me? I will not send my troops on a suicide attack. And I do not recognize your authority to order them to. I did not vote to place you in charge of the Alliance, and if Bail did, then he was gravely mistaken.''"
"''Senator Bel Iblis, I sincerely hope that was not an ultimatum.''"
"''Yes, I guess it is. This attack plan is fundamentally flawed, and you just don't have the expertise to know it.''"
"''I will not have ultimatums put to me, Senator. The Rebel Alliance will no longer require your services.|Garm Bel Iblis, and Mon Mothma|{{WEGCite|story=Differences of Opinion|book=The Last Command Sourcebook|reprint=1}}}} Not long after the incident at Anchoron, Bel Iblis suffered another blow, as his Senatorial ally Bail Organa perished in the destruction of Alderaan. Organa had long been a moderating influence between Bel Iblis and the emergent leader of the Rebellion, [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]]. But without him, tensions started to flare in the months after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. A superior military tactician, Bel Iblis questioned Mothma's strategic decisions and authority as [[Supreme_Commander_(Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the Alliance forces. The breaking point was reached on the eve of a [[Attack on Milvayne|raid]] on an Imperial base at [[Milvayne/Legends|Milvayne]]. Mothma had given approval for the attack without seeking the consent of Bel Iblis or any of her other military advisors. [[File:GarmBelIblis-SWR.png|thumb|left|200px|Garm Bel Iblis led his own private army against the Galactic Empire.]] In a tense exchange, Bel Iblis questioned Mothma's motives in ordering the attack, claiming it was simply part of a grab for power on her part. But he had material reasons for his objection, too, claiming that his informants conveyed that the purported Milvayne garrison Mothma was planning to attack was in fact a heavily fortified Imperial [[Ubiqtorate/Legends|Ubiqtorate]] base. Neither Mothma's nor his own troops were prepared to deal with such military might, he argued, and asserted that none of his troops would have a part in the attack. Understanding Bel Iblis' position as an ultimatum, Mon Mothma dismissed him from the Rebel Alliance. ===Private war against the Empire=== Although Bel Iblis withdrew from the Rebel Alliance with most of his personnel,''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' including his long-time aide [[Sena Leikvold Midanyl|Sena]], his rebel group was undermanned and spent its early years on the run from the Empire. Their headquarters, which Bel Iblis dubbed ''[[Peregrine's Nest]]'', had to be kept mobile and changed location fourteen times. On [[Stoga]], their outpost got infiltrated by Imperial spy [[Rasha Bex]] and had to be abandoned before the Imperial onslaught.[[Empire 23|''Empire'' 23]] As Bel Iblis quietly put out a call for soldiers, a former mercenary platoon led by [[Irenez (Human)|Irenez]] would join his resistance movement. Irenez, who had known Bel Iblis since the days the then-Senator had sponsored her admission to the [[Republic Military Academy]] on Corellia, quickly became Bel Iblis' most capable commander. [[File:Harrier.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The ''[[Harrier]]'', one of Bel Iblis' cruisers used in the raid on Tangrene.]] Hamstrung by a lack of capital ships and military equipment, Bel Iblis's movement spent its first three years [[hit-and-hype raiding]] small Imperial bases and military supply [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]], but without causing much of a concern to the Empire. Things changed, however, when Bel Iblis acquired a fleet of several (ultimately six) [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] from a smuggler named [[Hoffner]]. Unbeknownst to Bel Iblis, Hoffner had salvaged these ships from the [[Katana fleet|''Katana'' fleet]]. With the newly gained firepower, Bel Iblis set out on a daring [[Raid on Tangrene|raid]] of a major [[Ubiqtorate/Legends|Ubiqtorate]] center on [[Tangrene]]. In a decisive victory, his forces managed to evade three [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] guarding the system and completely destroyed the Ubiqtorate base, all while suffering only minimal casualties. The stunning success of the Tangrene mission caught the attention of [[Borsk Fey'lya]] of the [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]], who established a supply line to Bel Iblis in hopes of securing him as an ally. Bel Iblis, however, refused to be drawn into the internal politics of the Alliance. ===Lying in wait=== [[File:GarmBelIblis.jpg|thumb|right|210px|Han Solo and Lando Calrissian are brought before Bel Iblis at ''Peregrine's Nest''.]] After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Bel Iblis decided to cut back his attacks against the Empire while preparing for a strike against Mon Mothma instead, expecting that any moment she would turn the New Republic into a dictatorship. ''Perigrine's Nest'' was moved to the [[Churba sector/Legends|Churba sector]] to be close to New Republic space, and all raids except those for materials and supplies were suspended. Years passed, and ''Peregrine's Nest'' became permanent, until in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] arrive with a request for Bel Iblis' help against the threat of Grand Admiral [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. ==Appearances== *[[Empire 23|''Empire'' 23]] *''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' {{Ab|Dark Force Rising (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Dark Force Rising (abridged audiobook)|''Dark Force Rising'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Vision of the Future (abridged audiobook)|''Vision of the Future'' abridged audiobook]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *{{FactFile|94|YAV 13-16|The Evacuation of Yavin}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Garm Bel Iblis' Guerillakrieg |es=Movimiento de Garm Bel Iblis }} [[Category:Resistance groups]]
